mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k new jobs per month signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 1% indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods when INDPRO growth exceeds 1%, or the predictive power of INDPRO declines below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and stable inflation (CPI below 2%) signals a balanced economic expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances where both GDPC1 growth exceeds 0.5% and CPIAUCSL remains below 2%, or the interaction term between GDP and CPI loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and falling CPIAUCSL below 2% leads to higher discount rates and reduced equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is below 2%, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 800k units signals strong consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods when HOUST exceeds 800k, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE < 7%) indicates strong economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances where RSAFS increases and UNRATE falls below 7%, or the interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE loses significance (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment below 7% signals tight labor market conditions that push wages higher, boosting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods when UNRATE falls below 7%, or the predictive power of UNRATE declines below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.
